pattern: A triangular flow simulation: model the amount that reaches each cup, cap it at one unit, and split any overflow evenly into the two cups below.
hints: Track how much liquid arrives at every cup row by row, not just whether it is full.
A cup keeps at most one unit; whatever exceeds that splits in half and pours into the two cups directly beneath it.
Simulate row by row down to the queried row; the requested cup's fullness is its arrived amount clamped between zero and one.

edge_inputs.description: single pour reaches only the top cup, nothing below
exactly enough overflow to half-fill the second row
huge pour saturates a deep cup completely
nothing poured leaves the top empty
top cup queried directly with a large pour stays clamped at one
outer cup of a row gets only a quarter of the cascaded overflow
middle cup of the third row collects from both parents
deepest row with a moderate pour stays empty

stress.description: random pour amount with a queried cup chosen inside a valid mid-depth row
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    poured = rng.randint(0, 1000000000)
    row = rng.randint(0, 60)
    glass = rng.randint(0, row)
    return [poured, row, glass]
stress.note: row bounded so the simulation stays quick while exercising deep cascades
